romanized: Yīng shì zǎocān chá ná tiě

region_of_origin: United Kingdom (served globally via café chains)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for 'English Breakfast Tea Latte'. FOODON_03301339 (tea beverage) is the closest defensible parent class. A more specific term for milk-tea latte preparations does not exist in the current FoodOn release.
ontology.foodex2_description: Tea, infusions and other hot beverages
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN000AJ covers tea and hot beverage infusions as a category. No specific code exists for English Breakfast Tea Latte as a milk-based tea latte preparation. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 74242007 represents tea as a substance. No specific concept exists for English Breakfast Tea Latte as a prepared milk-tea beverage. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard tall serving of English Breakfast Tea Latte is approximately 355 g (12 fl oz), consisting of brewed English Breakfast black tea combined with steamed whole milk and a small amount of sweetener.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Tazo English Breakfast Tea Latte — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are derived from USDA FoodData Central branded food entries for Starbucks-style English Breakfast Tea Latte (tall, whole milk, standard sweetener). Values are expressed per 100 g of the prepared beverage. The beverage is approximately 88–90% water by weight. Core macros are cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy entries for brewed black tea (FDC ID 14355) and whole milk (FDC ID 01077) to validate the blended composition. Energy calculated as: protein × 4 + carbohydrate × 4 + fat × 9.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of milk and milk-based beverages; black tea GI reference — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for English Breakfast Tea Latte as a commercial preparation. GI is estimated at 35 using the University of Sydney GI Database analogous references: whole milk GI ≈ 27–34 (low), black tea has negligible glycemic effect, and added sugar (sucrose) GI ≈ 65. The blended GI of approximately 35 reflects the dominant milk fraction (~70% of volume) combined with a modest sugar contribution (~6.5 g sugar per 100 g). Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (35 × 24.85) / 100 ≈ 8.7 for a 355 g serving (carbohydrate_g per serving = 7.0 × 3.55 = 24.85 g).

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~35 mg per 100 g; ~124 mg per tall serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single tall serving contributes approximately 124 mg sodium, which is well within the daily limit of 2000 mg recommended by the Singapore Health Promotion Board for hypertensive individuals; however, patients should account for cumulative sodium from all dietary sources throughout the day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar and moderate carbohydrate load (~24.9 g carbohydrate per tall serving, GL ≈ 8.7)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic load of approximately 8.7 per tall serving and approximately 24.9 g of carbohydrate (predominantly from added sugar and milk lactose), individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request reduced or no added syrup and consider using low-fat or plant-based milk alternatives to lower the overall glycemic and caloric impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content (~1.2 g per 100 g; ~4.3 g per tall serving) from whole milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat contribution of approximately 4.3 g per tall serving is moderate relative to the recommended daily limit of less than 20 g for cardiovascular risk reduction; individuals with cardiovascular disease may benefit from substituting whole milk with a semi-skimmed or plant-based alternative to reduce saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: English Breakfast Tea Latte is a café-style hot beverage made by combining a strong brew of English Breakfast black tea — a robust blend typically comprising Assam, Ceylon, and Kenyan teas — with steamed whole milk and a small amount of sweetener such as simple syrup. The result is a smooth, creamy, lightly sweetened drink with a bold tea character softened by the richness of milk. It is served hot and is popular as a morning or afternoon beverage in café chains such as Starbucks across Singapore and globally. Per 100 g, the beverage provides approximately 52 kcal, 1.8 g protein, 1.9 g fat (of which 1.2 g is saturated), and 7.0 g carbohydrate (predominantly sugars at 6.5 g). The beverage is approximately 88.5% water by weight. It has a low glycemic index of approximately 35 and a moderate glycemic load of approximately 8.7 per tall serving (355 ml), making it a lower-GI choice compared to many sweetened café beverages. Clinically, the primary considerations are the added sugar content for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes and the saturated fat from whole milk for those with cardiovascular risk. Sodium content is low. The beverage contains milk and is therefore not suitable for those with dairy allergies or lactose intolerance. It is vegetarian and halal-friendly. In Singapore, it is commonly ordered at Starbucks outlets and is priced at approximately SGD 7.90 per serving.

confidence_reason: No exact branded nutritional entry for this specific Starbucks Singapore product was available in an approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central branded food entries for comparable Starbucks-style English Breakfast Tea Latte preparations and cross-validated against USDA SR Legacy entries for brewed black tea and whole milk. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using milk and sugar GI references. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are expressed per 100 g of the prepared beverage (whole milk, standard sweetener, brewed English Breakfast black tea). 2. Serving size of 355 g corresponds to a standard Starbucks tall (12 fl oz) cup. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero: brewed black tea and whole milk contain no dietary fibre; this is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for this beverage category. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero: black tea and whole milk do not contribute meaningful vitamin C; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy. 6. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central branded entry for this beverage type. 7. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central branded entry for this beverage type. 8. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central branded entry for this beverage type. 9. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central branded entry for this beverage type. 10. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central branded entry for this beverage type. 11. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central branded entry for this beverage type. 12. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central branded entry for this beverage type. 13. glycemic_index is set to 35 as an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for English Breakfast Tea Latte exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 14. Multilingual names follow the Singaporean dish convention (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this food is served in Singapore via field survey. 15. Hindi alias 'इंग्लिश टी लाटे' is a shortened colloquial form; the full form 'इंग्लिश ब्रेकफास्ट टी लाटे' is the primary Hindi local name. 16. Price sourced from field survey data input record: SGD 7.90 (single price point, one restaurant: Starbucks One Fullerton).
